title: codex-update wire shape additions
description: Documents the field additions to provider request input and the resulting HTTP headers / WS request body. Only delta against current provider state is captured here; unchanged fields are out of scope.

BuildHeadersOptionsDelta.description: Additive option on packages/opencode-codex-provider/src/headers.ts BuildHeadersOptions. All existing fields preserved; only new field listed here.

threadId.description: Per-thread identifier. Distinct from sessionId per upstream codex commit a98623511b. When omitted, defaults to sessionId so single-thread callers keep current wire behavior. Used as the value for the thread_id HTTP header, x-client-request-id HTTP header, and prompt_cache_key body field.

EmittedHttpHeadersDelta.description: Headers emitted by buildHeaders() that change as a result of this plan. Other headers unchanged.

session_id.description: Unchanged; always sourced from sessionId option.

thread_id.description: NEW. Sourced from threadId option (defaults to sessionId). Mirrors upstream build_session_headers second argument.

x-client-request-id.description: CHANGED source. Was sourced from conversationId; now sourced from threadId (defaults to sessionId so default behavior unchanged). Aligns with upstream codex-rs/core/src/client.rs:871.

WsErrorReasonAdditions.description: NEW value added to the wsErrorReason union in transport-ws WS observer state. Existing values (first_frame_timeout, mid_stream_stall, etc.) preserved.

EmptyTurnClassifierTransientReasons.description: Existing transient-failure reason set in empty-turn-classifier.ts. ws_send_timeout is added to this set; other entries unchanged.

deltas.to: thread-derived (defaults to session, no behavioral change for single-thread callers)

deltas.behavior: wrap with promise + idle-timeout race; on timeout, set wsErrorReason='ws_send_timeout' and abort

no-change-fields.reason: upstream type changed enum→string but wire values identical; provider already uses string (types.ts:19)
untouched upstream; backend handles compaction internally

deferred-features.reason: server-flag-gated; no public way to detect activation; revisit when activation observed
provider relies on backend-internal compact via context_management body field; no client surface to integrate
